interactions.summary: Jiang answers the spy/terror/control and Satoshi questions by describing imperial sponsorship of Islamic extremism and then using game-theory questions to identify the American deep state or CIA as the likely blockchain beneficiary.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Interview exchange: SPEAKER_00 is host Jack Neel or ad read; SPEAKER_01 is Jiang unless noted.

speaker_notes.note: Diarization is consistent enough for this packet: SPEAKER_00 carries host questions, intro, outro, or sponsor reads; SPEAKER_01 carries Jiang answers. Sponsor ad segments are not Jiang claims.

claims.claim: Jiang claims Anglo-American empire encouraged Islamic extremism to sabotage the Soviet Union, counter Middle Eastern nationalism/socialism, and destabilize the region in ways that benefited Israel.
Jiang says game-theory analysis of Bitcoin asks who had the expertise, who benefited, and why secrecy mattered, leading him to the American deep state/CIA as the likely creator or sponsor.

signature_moments.moment: Blockchain becomes a Trojan horse for surveillance and black-budget finance.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This is the memorable turn in his Satoshi theory.

signature_moments.moment: The value of Bitcoin depends on people believing it is beyond authority.
signature_moments.source_phrase: only if people believe that this was transparent, open, and beyond authority
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It explains why secrecy is structurally necessary in the theory.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: Jiang's method of asking capability, beneficiary, and secrecy questions to infer hidden actors.
A technology Jiang says looks open while enabling monitoring and financing.

chronology_notes.note: These are speculative claims dated 2026-04-15; Jiang does not cite confidential evidence here.

uncertainty_notes.note: Several claims in this packet are explicitly speculative or conspiratorial in Jiang's framing; preserve attribution to this interview rather than promoting them as established fact.
